info.title: Viewpoint API v1
info.description: The Viewpoint API provides access to data from the Viewpoint Construction ERPs (Enterprise Resource Planning) software, Vista and Spectrum. Trimble's API's are built to link our construction ERPs with other systems used in the construction industry. The fundamental focus for a construction company is to manage the work and costs necessary to complete a job, which might be referred to as a project. For access to the Trimble Viewpoint APIs, email integration-jumpstart-ug@trimble.com with the enterprise that you would like to use and the Trimble ID associated with your application. The endpoints are sorted alphabetically. The default output page contains 20 records. Data that can be pulled from the ERPs includes but is not limited to company, contract, employee, equipment, project cost, vendor

post.description: Creates new Equipment for the enterprise. Vista requires CategoryId, DepartmentId, and StatusId. Spectrum requires CategoryId (Type) and StatusId. Spectrum sometimes requires Division and CostCenter. When entering ownershipStatus, valid entries are (O)wned, (L)eased, (R)ented or (C)ustomer.

Creates new Equipment Location Transfer for the enterprise. If the equipment is getting transferred to a project/job use 'destinationType': 'JOB'. If the equipment is getting transferred to a location like a warehouse use 'destinationType': 'LOCATION'.

post.summary: Endpoint to create Unapproved Invoices in bulk
post.description: Creates new unapproved invoices for the enterprise

post.summary: Endpoint to create Daily Production records. Spectrum calls them Job Quantity Complete. Vista calls them Progress Entry.
post.description: Creates new daily production records for the enterprise
Creates new purchase orders for the enterprise

Bearer.description:
JWT Authorization header using the Bearer scheme.
Enter 'Bearer <access token>' in the text input below.
Bearer.name: Authorization

tags.name: Access Endpoints
tags.description: Access refers to users and their access to data from an enterprise. An Enterprise is defined as a Viewpoint Customer.

tags.name: Action Endpoints
tags.description: An Action refers to what happened to the data in the API such as 'created' which means the record was created in the ERP.

tags.name: Company Endpoints
tags.description: A company is an entity within the ERP that contains Customers, Contracts, Jobs, Employees, Equipment, Vendors, and other objects necessary to conduct business.

tags.name: Contract Endpoints
tags.description: A Contract is the agreement between the Company and Customer for work on a Customer's Job or Project.  In some ERP's, multiple Contracts can be tied to a single Job, in other ERPs, multiple Jobs can be tied to a single Contract. A Contract is different from a Subcontract. A Subcontract is an agreement between the Company and a Vendor.

tags.name: Customer Endpoints
tags.description: A Customer is the person or group that pays the Company for work done on the Job.

tags.name: Employee Endpoints
tags.description: An Employee is a person who works for the Company.

tags.name: Enterprise Endpoints
tags.description: An Enterprise is defined as a Viewpoint Customer.

tags.name: Equipment Category Endpoints
tags.description: Equipment Categories are used to separate different types of equipment.

tags.name: Equipment Department Endpoints
tags.description: Equipment Departments are assigned and control cost and revenue posting in the ERP.

tags.name: Equipment Endpoints
tags.description: Equipment is a non-consumable asset that is used on the Job or project.  Equipment could be heavy machinery such as bulldozers and loaders, or could be tools such as a reciprocating saw. Equipment costs, like maintenance and repair, are tracked in the ERP, and the Equipment usage is charged to the jobs as a cost to the job.

tags.name: Equipment Location Endpoints
tags.description: This refers to the location where the asset or equipment is. This could be a Job or a Yard where it sits between jobs.

tags.name: Equipment Location Transfer Endpoints
tags.description: The event that hands off a piece of equipment between one job site and another or between a storage location and a job site. The Equipment Location Transfer is used by the ERP to determine charge amounts to the Job for how long the asset was at the jobsite. Occasionally the Location Transfer will not indicate a physical move, but just end the charge to the Job.

tags.name: Equipment Status Endpoints
tags.description: Equipment Statuses refer to the operational status of the equipment.

tags.name: Inventory Item Endpoints
tags.description: Consumables needed for a Job or Project, such as wood, nails, paint, concrete etc.

tags.name: Inventory Location Endpoints
tags.description: An Inventory Location is a warehouse or other location used to house Inventory Items.

tags.name: Project Cost Entry Endpoints
tags.description: Project Cost Entry Endpoints allow entry of production and costs associated with projects/jobs.

tags.name: Project Cost History Endpoints
tags.description: The history provides a list of transactions charged to a Job for Cost, Hours, and Quantity. Transactions in Project Cost History are posted from accounts payable, payroll, inventory, equipment control, and other sources. Project Cost History does not show unposted transactions such as payroll in process, or AP invoices not yet approved.

tags.name: Project Endpoints
tags.description: A Project is the object around which the Company organizes its work. Projects are also known as Jobs. Projects are broken down into Phases, and any cost data applied to a Job must also include a Phase Code and Cost Type.

tags.name: Project Phase Endpoints
tags.description: Projects are broken down into Phases, also known as Cost Codes, Task Codes, or WBS Codes. Project Phases are those phases actually assigned to a Job, either from the list of Standard Phases or made up specifically for the Job. Project Phases are uniquely identified by Company, Job, Phase Code, and Cost Type.

tags.name: Purchase Order endpoints
tags.description: A Purchase Order is an order issued to a vendor for materials or services for Projects or Inventory replenishment.

tags.name: Sales Tax Endpoints
tags.description: Cost due to state tax requirements in addition to the contract cost that is paid to the enterprise.

tags.name: Schedule Of Values Endpoints
tags.description: The SOV is a list of deliverables for a Project, the Schedule of Values is attached to the Contract. The Company bills the Customer for work completed as detailed in the Schedule of Values.

tags.name: Standard Cost Type Endpoints
tags.description: Cost Types are fundamental Job cost inputs such as labor, materials, equipment, and others.

tags.name: Standard Phase Endpoints
tags.description: A Phase is a list of work to be performed to complete the project. The Phase is sometimes called a Cost Code, or Task Code, or WBS Code.  The Standard Phase list defines a standard phase structure to be used across all Jobs. Not all ERPs require the standard phase structure to be used.

tags.name: Subcontract endpoints
tags.description: A Subcontract is a contract issued to a vendor for services in support of a Project.

tags.name: Unapproved Invoice Endpoints
tags.description: An Unapproved Invoid is an Acoounts Payable (AP) invoice entered into the system but not yet posted to the general ledger.

tags.name: Unit of Measure Endpoints
tags.description: Unit of Measure is a factor to express occurring quantities. Cubic Yards, Square Feet, Each, Tons are examples of Unit of Measure. Unit of Measure is most commonly used when describing the quantity of materials billed to a Job, and when production quantities are reported.

tags.name: Vendor Alternate Address Endpoints
tags.description: A Vendor Alternate Address is an address for the Vendor other than the primary address.  These could be separate physical locations or order or remittance addresses.

tags.name: Vendor Endpoints
tags.description: A Vendor is a person or Company providing a service to the Company.
